algorithms.title: Newborn Resuscitation Initial Sequence
algorithms.description: Field sequence for assessing and stabilizing a newborn immediately after delivery.
steps: Ask the three initial questions: Term gestation? Good tone? Breathing or crying?
If all three are yes: keep the newborn with the mother for routine care (dry, warm, skin-to-skin).
If any answer is no: move the newborn to a warmer.
Perform the initial steps over about 30 seconds: provide warmth, position the airway, clear secretions only if needed, dry thoroughly, and stimulate.
If breathing well and heart rate ≥100 bpm: continue routine care and monitoring.
If apneic/gasping or heart rate <100 bpm: begin positive-pressure ventilation (PPV).
Reassess heart rate after 30 seconds of effective PPV; if <60 bpm, begin chest compressions coordinated with PPV.
For detailed medication dosing and advanced steps, follow local neonatal resuscitation protocol (NRP).
